sections.description: lil-olt-metrics is a minimal, single-process metrics server that ingests OTLP metrics via gRPC and HTTP, stores them in pluggable backends, and serves them via the Prometheus HTTP API with an embedded Vue.js SPA dashboard for visualizing Claude Code metrics.
sections.purpose: Provide a lightweight, self-contained metrics aggregation and querying system for OTLP-based observability workflows. Act as a bridge between OTel metric sources and Prometheus-compatible monitoring stacks like Grafana, enabling metrics collection without heavyweight infrastructure. Includes an embedded Vue.js + Vite SPA dashboard for visualizing Claude Code session metrics.
strengths: Zero-config startup with sensible defaults; runs as single binary with no external dependencies
Full OTLP feature support: all metric types (Gauge, Sum, Histogram, ExponentialHistogram, Summary), protobuf and JSON, gzip compression, delta-to-cumulative conversion
Complete Prometheus API compatibility including instant/range queries, series, labels, metadata, buildinfo endpoints; PromQL engine embedded and functional
Multiple pluggable storage backends: built-in filesystem with WAL and block compaction, Prometheus remote write/read, VictoriaMetrics import/export
Production-ready: data retention policies (age/size), graceful shutdown, gRPC/HTTP error handling, structured logging with slog, CORS middleware
Well-organized codebase following Go best practices: unexported by default, dependency injection, interfaces at consumer site, moq-generated mocks, parallel tests with t.Parallel()
Embedded Vue.js SPA dashboard served at /dashboard/ with activity heatmap, token usage, cost breakdown, cache efficiency, Chart.js visualizations, and auto-refresh
Multi-stage build pipeline: Vite frontend build embedded via Go embed.FS, Docker multi-stage with Node.js + Go stages
Platform-specific install scripts for macOS and Linux that build from source
weaknesses: Single-process design limits horizontal scalability; not suitable for high-cardinality distributed metrics at scale
Filesystem storage backend lacks distributed clustering; remote backends (Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics) required for HA
Delta-to-cumulative conversion with TTL eviction may lose state during restarts
Limited metric cardinality handling compared to production-grade systems; memory footprint grows with unique label combinations
sections.test_coverage: Comprehensive test suite across all packages. cmd/server: integration with graceful shutdown, flag parsing. internal/config: YAML loading, defaults, env var override. internal/ingest: OTLP gRPC/HTTP handlers, protobuf/JSON parsing, translator logic. internal/store: filesystem WAL/compaction, Prometheus remote write/read, VictoriaMetrics. internal/query: Prometheus API endpoints, dashboard redirect and SPA serving. internal/integration: end-to-end tests with telemetry generation. All Go tests use t.Parallel(), t.Context(), moq mocks, structured logging. Frontend: Vitest tests for utility functions (format.ts, heatmap.ts).
dependencies: prometheus/prometheus v0.309.1 — PromQL engine and data model
Frontend: Vue 3.5, Vite 6, Chart.js 4, chartjs-adapter-date-fns 3, TypeScript 5.7, Vitest 3
Dev: golangci-lint v2.9.0, moq v0.6.0, testify v1.11.1, gofumpt v0.9.2, vue-tsc
sections.functionality: 1. Ingestion: gRPC (port 4317) and HTTP (port 4318) endpoints accept OTLP ExportMetricsServiceRequest. 2. Translation: Converts OTLP metric types to Prometheus samples, applies unit suffixes, maps resource attributes to labels, records schema URLs. 3. Storage: Samples written via Appender interface to configured backend (FS, Prometheus, VictoriaMetrics). 4. Query: Prometheus HTTP API (/api/v1/*) backed by embedded PromQL engine for instant/range queries, series metadata, label enumeration, metadata lookup. CORS support for browser-based clients. 5. Dashboard: Vue.js SPA built with Vite and TypeScript, embedded via Go embed.FS, served at /dashboard/. Visualizes Claude Code metrics — token usage by model/type, cost breakdown, cache efficiency, active time per session, activity heatmaps — with auto-refresh and configurable time ranges. 6. Configuration: Loaded from env vars (LOM_* prefix), YAML file, or defaults. 7. Lifecycle: Graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT, drain in-flight requests, close backends. 8. Installation: Universal install script downloads pre-built binaries from GH releases and sets up system services. 9. Website: Single-page documentation site deployed to GitHub Pages.
sections.files: Root: go.mod, go.sum (Go dependencies), Makefile (build/test/lint/fmt targets, frontend build), .golangci.yml (linter config), README.md, CHANGELOG.md, Dockerfile (multi-stage: Node.js frontend + Go builder + distroless runtime), docker-compose.yml, .gitignore. Subdirs: cmd/server/ (main.go entrypoint), internal/{config,ingest,query,store,integration,tools}/ (Go packages), web/ (Vue.js + Vite + TypeScript SPA), docs/ (architecture, API reference), plans/ (roadmap), scripts/ (install scripts), site/ (documentation website). CI/CD: .github/workflows/{ci.yml (lint/test/build with Node.js + Go), release.yml (frontend build + Go cross-compilation + publish), pages.yml (deploys site/ to GitHub Pages)}.
